summary: node-ipc 9.1.6 contains obfuscated sabotage payload targeting users by geolocation
details: Version 9.1.6 of node-ipc ships an obfuscated module (node-ipc.cjs, line ~1271 onward, using hex-identifier mangling like _0xaed59b, _0x282d65) that checks the host's public IP geolocation and, when the IP resolves to Russia or Belarus, recursively overwrites files on the user's filesystem with a heart emoji. The dependency was pulled in transitively by a very large number of downstream packages (notably via Vue CLI and other tooling), so any installer or CI system whose outbound IP resolved to the targeted geographies would have had arbitrary files on disk destroyed when the module loaded. The payload is obfuscated to hide this behavior from casual inspection, and the destructive action fires simply by requiring the module (e.g. during a normal build), not as a deferred runtime feature.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
